# Spec Quality Checklist: Core Food Ordering System

**Purpose**: Verify that the implemented system complies with all functional, security, and payment requirements defined in the specification.
**Created**: 2026-06-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Authentication & Authorization Requirements

- [ ] CHK001 User password hash uses bcryptjs with salt rounds of 10.
- [ ] CHK002 Stateless JWT authentication is used, with tokens stored securely on the client and sent via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` headers.
- [ ] CHK003 Middleware (`authGuard` and `adminGuard`) is implemented to intercept and validate JWTs before allowing access to protected API routes.
- [ ] CHK004 Client-side routing restricts access to the admin panel using a role-based check (e.g. `AdminRoute` component).

## Payment Integration & Webhook Validation

- [ ] CHK005 Checkout initiation backend generates required fields and calculates the secure MD5 signature hash matching PayHere specs.
- [ ] CHK006 Webhook listener (`notify_url` endpoint) parses and validates the PayHere MD5 signature before updating transaction state.
- [ ] CHK007 PayHere notifications update order status to "Preparing" and payment status to "Paid" inside a database transaction or atomic operation.
- [ ] CHK008 Redirect page `/confirmation` displays payment receipt information and active order status queried from the backend.

## Catalog & Order Flow Compliance

- [ ] CHK009 Orders compute the total amount on the server using prices stored in the database, ignoring client-side total calculations.
- [ ] CHK010 Order status transitions strictly adhere to: Pending -> Preparing -> Ready -> Delivered -> Cancelled.
- [ ] CHK011 Seeding script initializes the administrative user (`admin@food.com` / `admin123`) and default food item catalog correctly.

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
